oneLiner: An AI assistant that turns customer reviews into replies, retention tasks, social posts, and weekly business improvement insights.
targetUsers: Restaurants, salons, clinics, gyms, tutors, and local service businesses
Small business owners who manage reviews themselves
problem: Local businesses know reviews affect trust and sales, but owners do not consistently reply, detect repeat complaints, or reuse positive reviews as marketing content.
solution: Import or paste reviews, classify sentiment and issue type, generate brand-safe replies, flag urgent complaints, and create weekly improvement actions and social post ideas.
marketGap: Enterprise reputation tools are too expensive and agency-oriented. Generic AI chat can write replies but does not manage the full review-to-retention workflow.
originalValue: The product turns reviews into revenue actions: reply, recover customer, improve operations, and convert praise into marketing content.
revenueOpportunity: This sits in AI workflow and local business marketing. Businesses already pay monthly for reputation, social media, and customer retention tools.
whyNow: AI writing quality is strong enough for review replies, and local businesses increasingly need faster customer response across Google, social, and delivery platforms.

evidence.finding: Non-game app revenue and AI workflow apps are growing strongly.
Local businesses already pay for marketing, reviews, and agency support.
Agency dashboards and multi-location workflows can raise LTV.

competitors.name: Enterprise reputation platforms
competitors.weakness: Too expensive or complex for small businesses
competitors.opportunity: Simple self-serve AI product for owner-operators

competitors.name: Generic AI chat tools
competitors.weakness: No review inbox, classification, history, or business insights
competitors.opportunity: Own the workflow around replies and improvement actions

goals: Help owners reply to reviews in minutes.
Turn positive reviews into reusable marketing content.
nonGoals: Full social media management in v1
mvpFeatures: Social post draft from positive reviews
userStories: As a salon owner, I want quick review replies so customers feel heard.
As a restaurant owner, I want repeated complaints grouped so I can fix operations.
As an agency, I want to generate drafts for multiple clients quickly.
functionalRequirements: The app must let users paste review text.
The app must classify review sentiment and topic.
The app must generate editable replies.
The app must show a weekly summary of recurring issues.
The app must require user review before publishing any response.
nonFunctionalRequirements: Replies should be generated in under 10 seconds.
The app must support brand tone settings.
The app must store source review and generated reply history.
successMetrics: At least 10 reviews processed per active business per month
Weekly active usage by business owners
validationPlan: Interview 10 local businesses with public reviews.
Manually create review reports for 5 businesses.
Test willingness to pay for $9, $19, and $49 monthly tiers.
risks: Platform API access may be limited.
Bad AI replies could damage customer relationships.
Some businesses may prefer agencies over software.
openQuestions: Which local business niche should be targeted first?
Should this launch as mobile app, web app, or both?
